programme.title: Fred West: The Glasgow Girls

programme.desc: Observing the natural and human forces that have shaped this area, discovering a million-year-old fossil reef, a gargantuan fish and a ray that packs quite a punch.

programme.title: Ningaloo: Australia's Ocean Wonder

programme.title: Ningaloo: Australia's Ocean Wonder
programme.desc: How Ningaloo's conservation success could inspire hope for the future and help bring the planet back from the brink of climatic destruction.

programme.title: Wild Rockies
programme.desc: A look at how some creatures manage to defy gravity among the highest peaks on the continent, where the air is thin and cold and even trees cannot survive.

programme.desc: Documentary about a group of elusive chimpanzees living in the Fongoli region of Senegal, on the southern edge of the Sahara Desert. It's the hottest, driest place in the world that chimps can live and they really shouldn't be here - so how do they survive?

programme.desc: Dan and Jess tackle mating eastern browns in a backyard. Mick also finds a venomous snake in couch cushions.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: The Builders give a modern mansion a complete log cabin makeover complete with log skins and a stacked log entryway; the home will be the centerpiece for a log cabin community; Mark tours a restored and redesigned pioneer cabin.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: The Barnwood Builders fight through mud and heat to turn the salvaged Shaver House into a two-story lake house in Alabama; Mark experiments with new construction products; everyone ends up in the lake.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: In Jane Lew, W. Va., Mark and the guys salvage the wood from a 120-year-old cattle barn that is slated for demolition; the site is so wet they have to build their own road just to get to the barn.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: In West Virginia, Mark and the guys salvage rare 170-year-old logs from a perfectly preserved log home; the pressure is on to keep these logs pristine as they take them down.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: Mark and the guys volunteer to help a boy scout troop build a new lodge out of their old lodge while teaching them about the pioneer life.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: In New England, Mark and the guys dismantle a 200-year-old barn; the barn is so big it takes extra hands to get the barn wood down and a crane to lift out the valuable beams.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A couple found an isolated cabin and fell in love with idea of owning and restoring it; Chase and the team have their hands full trying to repair and restore this cabin that's only accessible by hiking or by boating across the lake.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The Hamill family hope that Chase and the Cabin Masters can improve the livability of their rustic 1963 Mount Vernon cabin, which has no kitchen, bathroom or electricity.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A family of four from Florida is living in their dream home on a scenic Maine lake, but from the kitchen to the rafters, it's in need of some serious renovations to withstand the winter months and really work for them.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Kristin's family has owned this cabin for five generations, but now would like to add on to the kitchen and raise the roof to comfortably fit her husband and son, and add more space for their daughter to enjoy the space with ease.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase and the team transform a quaint cabin and its accompanying railroad caboose; they enlarge the main cabin with more sleeping space, indoor plumbing and an updated kitchen, and they create more bunk space and storage space in the caboose.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The Chadwick family has purchased a cabin built in 1906 and need the Cabin Masters help for a remodel; with an eight-week deadline, the team must fix a rotting ceiling, update the interior and create a custom outdoor bar and fire pit.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A woman's husband suddenly passed away before finishing their family cabin; Chase and the Cabin Masters team up with some green friends to make this family's perfect summer cabin dreams come true.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Gardiner Area High School is in need of an outdoor classroom for their earth sciences program, so they call on some of their notable Gardiner Tiger alumni; the crew builds the room to be an optimal learning space with plenty of storage.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The Harringtons purchased a tranquil plot of oceanside property in Phippsburg way back in the 1950s; Joe Harrington, a Maine Basketball Hall of Famer, cleared a parcel so he can construct a camp of his own to add to the family's legacy.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The crew acquires a retired lumberyard and builds out a new wood shop for projects; they also revamp their headquarters to improve flow.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Ryan and Ashley buy a rundown camp on their own island; the team boats over, ready to haul materials and tackle a tricky gray water system; but after demo, island life proves tough.
